figures.caption: Filtered PM2.5 annual 2019 records for California under the 'PM25 Annual 2006' standard, retaining site identifiers and arithmetic mean.
California county polygons enriched with 2019 county-level PM2.5 mean and monitoring site counts via GEOID left join.
California county polygons with Queen contiguity summary (neighbor counts) and centroid-to-centroid neighbor links keyed by GEOID.
Global Moran’s I (Queen contiguity; row-standardized) for county-level PM2.5 mean in 2019, reporting permutation-based variance, z-score, and p-value.
California counties with 2019 PM2.5 mean and 2019 ACS Gini Index joined by GEOID.
OLS coefficients for PM2.5 (2019) regressed on county Gini Index.
OLS fit statistics for PM2.5 (2019) ~ Gini Index model.
County-level OLS fitted PM2.5 (2019) and residuals from regression on Gini Index.
Global Moran’s I (permutation-based) for spatial autocorrelation in OLS residuals of PM2.5 across California counties using Queen contiguity.
Maximum-likelihood spatial lag (SAR) model results for county PM2.5 (2019) regressed on Gini Index with Queen contiguity weights (GEOID-keyed).
K-nearest-neighbor (k=5) spatial weights for counties, represented as centroid-to-centroid edges keyed by GEOID.
Spatial error (SEM) maximum-likelihood estimates for PM2.5 (2019) regressed on county Gini Index (KNN k=5 weights, keyed by GEOID).
County-level urban–rural classification fields with standardized 5-character county GEOID.
California county polygons with PM2.5 mean (2019), Gini Index, and Metro2013 classification attached via GEOID.
OLS coefficient table for PM2.5 (2019) regressed on Gini Index, Metro2013, and their interaction (gini_x_metro).
Maximum-likelihood spatial lag (SAR) model coefficients for county PM2.5 (2019) with Gini, Metro2013, and their interaction using Queen contiguity weights keyed by GEOID.
Spearman rank correlation between 2019 county PM2.5 and Gini Index, stratified by Metro2013 status, with sample sizes.
Map showing the coefficient of estimation for Gini Index regressed on PM2.5 across counties.
